Tests that a variable is undefined upon declaration.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that demonstrates the default initialization of a variable in JavaScript. It declares a variable `a` without assigning a value, which means `a` is automatically initialized to `undefined`. The script then uses an `assert` statement to verify that `a` is indeed `undefined`. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on illustrating the behavior of uninitialized variables in JavaScript.
